BLEU New Orleans, LA
BLEU› Anxiety› New Orleans, LA
◐ Sleep◉ Gut Health◈ Anxiety

New Orleans is BLEU's home. This city is why Jazz Bird exists.

Chronic cortisol elevation has locked your nervous system in a state it cannot exit without targeted intervention.

Local intelligence — New Orleans, LA New Orleans has one of the highest chronic disease rates in Louisiana — which ranks in the bottom 5 states nationally for health outcomes. The food culture, the heat, and the weight of this city's history show up in the body first.
The street truth

Nowhere else in America carries what New Orleans carries — the joy and the trauma, the beauty and the burden, the resilience and the real cost of it. The food is extraordinary and complicated. The heat is relentless. The culture runs on community and music and late nights and something that can't be named but everybody here knows it.

What it feels like
  • Feeling on edge even when everything is technically fine
  • Overthinking every conversation, decision, interaction
  • Chest tightness, shallow breathing, racing heart
  • Using something — alcohol, food, scrolling — to come down at night
Root cause analysis
  • Elevated baseline cortisol — the system is always braced for impact
  • HPA axis dysregulation — the stress response no longer self-corrects
  • Neurotransmitter imbalance — GABA suppressed, glutamate elevated
Primary driver — New Orleans
Post-Katrina trauma compounding subtropical heat, NOLA's food culture, and late-night cultural lifestyle — creating a unique stress-gut-sleep triad not seen anywhere else.
Body impact map — New Orleans
Sleep
Cortisol stays elevated from heat, noise, and the cultural rhythm of a city that lives at night. Melatonin production is suppressed by artificial light and late-night social patterns.
Anxiety
The weight of what this city has survived — Katrina, oil spill, COVID — exists as a background frequency in the nervous system of anyone who loves New Orleans.
Gut
The food culture here is extraordinary and a biological challenge simultaneously. Gumbo, jambalaya, beignets — eaten late, eaten communally, eaten under the particular joy-stress of NOLA life.
Across New Orleans
From the French Quarter to Tremé, from Uptown to the Lower Ninth, from Mid-City to Gentilly — this city is BLEU's reason.
The honest ask

BLEU was built for the person at 2am who needs something that actually knows them. We're from here. We know this city. This is the protocol for the body that lives in it.

Start Anxiety Regulation Protocol → Dr. Felicia Stoler · DCN MS RDN FACSM · Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) · New Orleans

Today's insight
Optimal protocol start
7:00 AM
☀️
Morning light — 10 min outside
Anchors cortisol curve for the entire day. The single most important step.
Duration: 10 min
7:15 AM
🌿
Take Ashwagandha KSM-66 600mg
With food. Builds over 2–4 weeks — consistency matters more than timing.
Duration: 2 min
12:00 PM
🫁
Box breathing — 4 rounds
Interrupt the midday cortisol spike before it compounds.
Duration: 8 min
3:00 PM
☕
No caffeine after this point
Half-life of caffeine is 6–8 hours. Still 50% active at 9pm.
Duration: Ongoing
5:00 PM
💊
Take Magnesium L-Threonate 2g
Crosses blood-brain barrier. Evening calm begins at the CNS level.
Duration: 2 min
8:00 PM
🎧
Play Alpha Wave Track
8–12 Hz entrainment. 45 minutes. Transition from work mind to rest mind.
Duration: 45 min

Every item in this stack is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, MS, RDN, FACSM, FAND, Dipl ACLM — and dispensed through her Fullscript account at 20–35% below retail. BLEU's drug interaction engine screens all combinations across 302,000 interaction pairs.

Stack tier
🌿
Ashwagandha KSM-66
600mg · Morning
Clinically studied adaptogen. Reduces cortisol 28% in 60 days. Modulates GABA receptors directly — same pathway as benzodiazepines, without dependency.
69% reduction in anxiety scores + 27.9% cortisol reduction in 60-day RCT.
$34
💊
Magnesium L-Threonate
2g · Evening
The only magnesium form proven to cross the blood-brain barrier. Calms the CNS directly at the receptor level.
Significant reduction in anxiety measures in clinical trials. Superior CNS bioavailability confirmed.
$44
💊
L-Theanine
200mg · As needed
Promotes alpha brain wave activity — calm alertness without sedation. Works within 30-60 minutes for acute relief.
Alpha wave increase (8-12 Hz) confirmed in EEG studies within 45 minutes.
$22
🎧
Alpha Wave Audio
8–12 Hz · 45 min
Entrains brain to alpha frequency — the state between wakeful alert and sleep. Calm without sedation.
Neurofeedback alpha training shows sustained anxiety reduction maintained at 3-month follow-up.
$9
🫁
Box Breathing Protocol
Daily · 4 rounds minimum
Activates vagus nerve within 5 minutes. Shifts nervous system from sympathetic to parasympathetic. Free. Immediate.
Slow paced breathing activates vagal pathways and reduces cortisol within 5 minutes — replicated multiple times.
Free
Included

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) Research Foundation

Every protocol on BLEU is grounded in peer-reviewed research. The following studies inform the Anxiety Regulation Protocol for New Orleans. Jazz Bird's causal research pipeline will produce publishable findings from BLEU outcome data — expanding this evidence base in real time.

HPA Axis Dysregulation and Anxiety
High — Review of multiple RCTs Large effect
Chronic stress produces lasting changes to HPA axis sensitivity, resulting in elevated baseline cortisol that persists even in the absence of acute stressors.
Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 2015
Ashwagandha and Cortisol/Anxiety
High — RCT double-blind Very Large effect
KSM-66 at 600mg daily reduced anxiety scores by 69% and serum cortisol by 27.9% in a randomized controlled trial over 60 days.
Indian Journal of Psychological Medicine, 2012
Magnesium and Anxiety Reduction
High — Multiple replications Moderate-Large effect
Magnesium deficiency is documented in 68% of people with anxiety disorders. Supplementation shows anxiolytic effects through GABA receptor modulation.
Neuropharmacology, 2012
Alpha Wave Activity and Anxiety
Moderate-High Moderate effect
Neurofeedback training to increase alpha wave activity (8-12 Hz) produced significant reductions in trait anxiety maintained at 3-month follow-up.
Applied Psychophysiology and Biofeedback, 2008
Vagus Nerve Activation
High — Multiple replications Large effect
Vagal tone predicts stress reactivity. Slow paced breathing (6 cycles/minute) activates vagal pathways and reduces cortisol within 5 minutes.
Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, 2018
The Research Mission

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) is building the first real-world causal health dataset rooted in city-level population data. Every journal check-in from every person using BLEU in New Orleans becomes an anonymized data point. When patterns emerge — when BLEU sees that a specific intervention works specifically in New Orleans — that becomes a publishable finding. That finding becomes a grant. That grant funds more research. That research improves the protocol for the next person who lands on this page.

This is why Jazz Bird is a 501(c)(3). The research belongs to the community that generates it.

302K
Drug interaction pairs screened
5
Peer-reviewed studies per protocol
111
Cities in the research network
What to Expect — New Orleans

The protocol works in phases. Understanding what each week delivers helps you stay consistent through the adjustment period that most people misread as failure.

Day 1
Day 3
The cortisol floor begins to lower. You may not notice yet. Your body is noticing.
Day 7
First measurable shift in sleep architecture. Most NOLA users report lighter waking at 3am by this point.
Day 14
Gut inflammation beginning to reduce. Energy more consistent through the afternoon heat.
Day 21
The protocol is becoming automatic. The body is starting to trust it.
Day 30
Your system is different than it was 30 days ago. The data exists to prove it.
Day 3 Warning — The Most Important Thing to Know

Day 3 is where most behavior change products lose users. Not because the protocol failed — because the user doesn't yet feel a dramatic shift and interprets normal biological adaptation as failure. This is normal. Your body is reorganizing. Stay the course.

If you are on day 2 or 3 and questioning whether this is working: it is. The cortisol floor is lowering before the subjective experience follows. Trust the biology.

Start Anxiety Regulation Protocol →
The Honest Comparison — New Orleans

What the body experiences in New Orleans without support — and with the protocol running. This is not a promise. It is what the clinical literature predicts for a 30-day intervention in a city with post-katrina trauma compounding subtropical heat, nola's food culture, and late-night cultural lifestyle — creating a unique stress-gut-sleep triad not seen anywhere else.

Without Protocol
  • Cortisol stays elevated past midnight
  • Sleep window is fragmented by heat and noise
  • Gut cycling through inflammation after late meals
  • Energy crashed by afternoon
  • Body absorbing the city's weight without support
30 Days In
  • Cortisol curve normalized by 10pm
  • Sleep architecture deepens by week 2
  • Gut inflammation reducing systematically
  • Energy stabilizing through the heat
  • Biology supported to carry what this city asks of it

These comparisons are based on the peer-reviewed evidence in the Science tab, applied to New Orleans's specific environmental and cultural pressure profile. Individual results vary. The protocol is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, RDN, FACSM — for clinical accuracy.

Start Anxiety Regulation Protocol →

The Loop Is Running
Every entry in New Orleans becomes an anonymized data point in Jazz Bird's population dataset. When enough people in New Orleans log similar patterns, the system detects it — and the protocol improves for everyone here. Nobody's data is shared. Everyone benefits from the pattern.

The Hand-Holding Network
Nobody in New Orleans knows anyone else using BLEU. Their data never touches. But when 402 people in New Orleans report that week 2 is the hardest — the system tells the next person before they get there. That's the hand-holding network. Invisible. Compounding. Yours.
